tax: Placed at order rank, Aeolian Elementals is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a bounded-air body, moving or still, without the electrical charge of storm kin. A new family belongs here only if it shows a distinct air mode with its own draught or seep source and field signs.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a dust-laden spinning column or an invisible pool that kills flame. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — dust spiralling against the draught, or a flame that gutters in still air — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Aeolian Elementals occupies vertical shafts, draught galleries, and low still dips. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where air-pressure animates a channelled draught or a pocket of foul gas and recur with the draught resumes or the foul-gas seep refills, sustaining themselves on air-pressure and channelled draught or foul gas.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: members hide as ordinary air and rouse to intrusion, flinging crews or suffocating them. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: a bounded-air body, immunity to physical attack, and an enclosure or ventilation failure. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each family and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: flinging, lifting, and silent suffocation. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Aeolian Elementals matters because they make exposed ledges and low dips lethal in invisible ways. It lets survey reports sort air hazards by motion, so a crew knows whether to double-clip a ledge or test-flame a dip without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Aeolian Elementals is the order-rank grouping of air elementals whose bodies are moving or settled air without electrical charge within the True Elementals. Its members share a body of bounded air, spinning or pooling, dependent on draught or seep for coherence, and divide below into family groups by whether the air is fast and spinning or slow and settled. Aeolian elementals are the hardest to see, since their bodies are air itself, betrayed only by dust or dying flame.
